title: OpenClaw MCP tool calls should not stop at a hard 60-second timeout

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #61786.

task.summary: Diagnose why long-running MCP tool calls time out at 60 seconds even when the backend is still working.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm the hard timeout behavior, localize it to the MCP call timeout path, and propose an ops-level repair plan that allows long-running tools to finish.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: Long-running MCP tools are cut off at exactly 60 seconds even though the backend continues processing.
error_logs: backend tool process still running after the client timed out
reproduction_steps: Inspect the evidence around the fixed 60-second cutoff.
Explain what timeout path must change so longer MCP calls can complete.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models timeout diagnosis around the MCP SDK request limit.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic MCP timeout incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #61786.
